Football.title: Premier League club warned ‘it’s now or never’ for 53,000 stadium expansion – but faces major stumbling block
Football.content:
LEEDS UNITED director Peter Lowy has claimed the club must act quickly if they want to go ahead with a complete redevelopment of Elland Road.

The Whites have called on the government to support plans which could see the capacity of Elland Road increase from 37,645 to approximately 53,000 seats.





Lowy believes it will be a once-in-a-generation opportunity to redevelop the ground and the surrounding area.

The club director has also suggested it might never happen if the wheels are not in motion soon.

He said: “Leeds is ready for it. If we don’t get it done this time, it will never happen.

“It does need to be done in a timely manner though because people like myself and the capital that we have won’t sit around for 10 years because we can invest wherever we choose to invest.”



More than £10million has already been spent on the planning application, with more to come for the actual redevelopment.

Leeds are believed to have thousands of supporters on a season ticket waiting list, meaning an expansion of the stadium would be hugely beneficial financially.

Project leaders also claim that such a redevelopment will add £30m in gross value to the city of Leeds each year.

Planning permission for the stadium expansion was granted in January, but Lowy believes that regenerating the area surrounding Elland Road is just as important.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

He added: “A stadium of the size we’re going to build, and a club of our size, needs to be able to get people there quickly, easily and have them spend time there.

“We need a system where we can move people in and out simply and efficiently. Not only that, but that kind of transportation will open up the whole area of south Leeds.”

It was reported earlier this year that Lowy had discussed raising funds for a planned light rail system running from Leeds station to White Rose – including a stop at Elland Road – with Chancellor Rachel Reeves.

The Leeds director is now calling on the “political class” to contribute to the stadium and surrounding project, of which between £10m and £20m has already been raised for.

He said: “What we need is the political class to get its act together. There’s a lot of work to be done.



“It will take a number of years. But it’s 2026 and we’re used to long timelines.”

Football.title: Iconic former Premier League manager immediately starts new job after being removed as boss
Football.content:
PHIL BROWN has started a new job in football after being removed as manager of Peterborough Sports.

The iconic Premier League boss has stayed with the non-league side but taken on a new role.



Brown, 66, was last in charge of the National League North outfit on Saturday as they were beaten 3-0 by South Shields.



Peterborough Sports have failed to win any of their last seven games and have dropped into the relegation zone.

Brown has swapped his position in the dugout to take up the director of football role.

The former Derby County and Hull City manager was without a club for six months before joining the club in September.

Striker Michael Gash will take charge of the team until the end of the season, having been incharge before Brown’s arrival.

Chairman Tim Woodward has hailed Brown’s efforts despite the club’s difficult season.

He said: “I would like to personally thank Phil for his commitment and contribution in his time as manager in this extremely hard season.

“We find ourselves in a precarious position with 11 games to go.

SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“I know I can rely on everyone’s full support for Gashy and the whole team in what will be 11 cup finals to maintain our position in the National League North.”



Brown has managed up and down England‘s football ladder and is best remembered for the iconic half-time team talk at Hull.

The Tigers were 4-0 down at Manchester City in the Premier League back in 2008 when he made the decision to give the talk on the pitch in front of the travelling fans.

Star Jimmy Bullard poked fun at the moment the following season after scoring an equaliser at the Etihad Stadium.

Brown left Hull in 2010 and went on to have spells at Preston North End, Southend and Swindon Town.



Now in the sixth tier of English football, he has revealed that he needed time to adjust to the level.

He told BBC Look East: “Everybody remembers the highest level [with Hull].

“But if I did something here with Peterborough Sports, there’s no doubt in my mind it would be a great achievement,” he said.

“If I can keep Peterborough Sports in this division, first and foremost… if we survive this year, which is our first goal.



“Then it’s all about next year and can we get promoted? Can we move this club on?”

Football.title: Mikel Arteta fears old teammate Danny Welbeck will damage Arsenal’s title hopes
Football.content:
DANNY WELBECK is in the form of his life as he bids to dent the title dream of his old Arsenal team-mate Mikel Arteta.

The 35-year-old is way more dangerous now than during his injury-ravaged five-year spell in north London.





Indeed, Arteta yesterday praised Welbeck as the perfect No 9 which may make Thomas Tuchel take note ahead of naming his England squad later this month.

Scoring against the title-chasing Gunners tonight would certainly grab the German’s attention with the World Cup now less than 100 days away.

Welbeck’s stunning renaissance has been one of the recent feel-good stories of the Premier League.

Don’t forget, this is a man who helped win Manchester United their last title 13 years ago.



And the same striker who scored the goal against Leicester in 2016 that many thought would end Arsenal‘s own long drought in Arteta’s final season as a player.

Welbeck joined the Gunners for £16million from United in 2014 and Arteta was his club captain.

Asked yesterday about his impact, the Arsenal boss said: “We were teammates and he is an exceptional guy, a really professional guy.

“He had some difficult moments in his career and he really has had to show his real character and go against the odds.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“People were saying, ‘No, he cannot do it’ but he is doing it and probably better than ever.

“As a person, he was top. He was so good in and around the dressing room.

“Whether he was a starter, a finisher, when he had some months with injuries as well, he was always a really positive person around the place.

“And as a No 9, he has everything. He can hold the ball up, he can drive forward. Technically, he is very good and he can finish actions in the box in different ways.”



Arteta will be hoping Welbeck does not continue his stunning form for Brighton in tonight’s encounter.

He reached double figures in the Premier League for the first time in his career last term and he has done it again after his goal in the 2-1 win over Nottingham Forest on Sunday.

Welbeck’s form will surely have caught the eye of England coach Tuchel, who – aside from Harry Kane – is not exactly blessed with goalscorers.

Although he scored 16 times in 42 appearances, Welbeck has not played an international match since a 1-0 friendly win over Switzerland in Leicester in 2018.

Asked if the forward could make a late charge for the World Cup squad, Arteta said: “I don’t know if he wants to be there. But if he wants to be there, I would be very happy for him.”



Arteta even played a role in Welbeck’s arrival as, along with Per Mertesacker, he urged former boss Arsene Wenger to sign a striker on deadline day in 2014.

On September 1, the day the window shut, Wenger knew his phone would be switched off all day as he was meeting the Pope in Rome.

So he told club officials in the morning that Arsenal had to sign Welbeck at all costs.

Coincidentally, Welbeck was already at the Gunners’ training ground with Roy Hodgson‘s England squad so he had to walk just a few metres for a medical.



When Welbeck hit a hat-trick against Galatasaray a few weeks later, Arsenal fans thought they had the answer to their goalscoring issues.

But ultimately, it was injuries to both his knees that stopped him from achieving more.

He scored a late goal against Leicester in February 2016 which kept Arsenal in the title race though the Gunners’ opponents that day later delivered their shock Prem success.

Welbeck grabbed a famous winner in the FA Cup at former club United in 2015 and featured in Arsenal’s 2-1 win over Chelsea in the FA Cup final in 2017.

Yet it was no surprise when his Gunners career ended and he jumped over the hedge to join Watford, who train next door.



He has since been at Brighton for the last 5½ years and his form continues to astound.

Albion manager Fabian Hurzeler two years younger than Welbeck said: “It’s not about me, it’s about Danny Welbeck and it’s about the medical department. Because Danny is in his best shape physically and therefore he has more game minutes on the pitch.

“More game minutes on the pitch means you have more chances to score goals. You can get better into rhythm.

“If you always have small issues, small injuries, you always get out of the rhythm. Then you have to come back, then you have to find your rhythm again.



“Therefore, it’s really coming back to my medical department, to the guys who work in the shadows, and especially to Danny, who takes care of his body, who is a hard worker in every training session and therefore he deserves these kinds of statistics.

“He is a dream to coach. It’s important to have an exchange, a trustful exchange with the medical department and also together with Danny. It’s not like a one-way communication.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Welbeck’s recent goals have eased some of the pressure on Hurzeler, who needed the victories over Brentford and Forest after six games without a win.

And if he finds the net tonight, could well hurt the man he once shared a dressing room with at Arsenal.

Football.title: LaLiga club write desperate letter to pop legend Madonna asking her to return ‘emotional heritage of our club’
Football.content:
MUSIC star Madonna has received a desperate plea from a Spanish football club asking her to return a piece of “emotional heritage”.

LaLiga club Celta Vigo hosted the artist at their Estadio de Balaídos back on July 29, 1990, during her Blond Ambition World Tour.





The now 67-year-old wore a Celta Vigo shirt during her performance in front of 20,000 people.

Los Celestes, or Sky Blues, are now asking for the pop legend to bring the shirt home to enshrine it ahead of their clash with Real Madrid at the ground on March 8.

Club president Marián Mouriño issued an open letter to Madonna that was released by the club on social media, asking for the Material Girl to return the shirt.

In full, it read: “Dear Madonna, I am writing this letter because we need your help.



“On July 29, 1990, you performed at the stadium of Celta de Vigo, the football club of which I am president. That night, you wore our sky-blue jersey.

“That memory remains very much alive among our fans. The image of you in our shirt became legendary and is part of Celta’s history that is also written off the pitch.

“Many see this as a simple anecdote. But I like to think that nothing happens by chance.

“While it wasn’t the only football jersey you’ve worn on stage, that image has begun to shine in a different way as the years have passed.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“Because we now better understand what you were defending back then: questioning the established order and standing up to those who try to tell you what you can or cannot do.”

The letter continued: “In the club I preside over, we recognise ourselves in that way of being in the world.

“That is why we have the hope of finding this garment. This search is not a whim; it is an act of memory, it is the emotional heritage of our club.

“This coming Friday, March 6, we want to dedicate a gesture of affection to you from our home, the Balaídos stadium.

“It will take place starting at 8:45 PM, before the Celta vs. Real Madrid match, with the intention of affectionately asking you this question: Do you have it?



“If you know where it might be or want to help us, please contact us via private message.”

The letter was sent on Wednesday morning, and Madonna, who has more than 54 million followers across mainstream social media platforms including Instagram, Facebook, X and TikTok, has yet to issue a response.

Football.title: Liverpool receive Champions League boost as Galatasaray fans BANNED from clash
Football.content:
Liverpool receive Champions League boost as Galatasaray fans BANNED from clash

Liverpool will host Galatasaray in the second leg of their Champions League last 16 clash without any away fans present after UEFA hit the Turkish club with a ban

View 2 Images Liverpool were beaten by Galatarasay in the Champions League group phase, but will face them again in the last 16

Liverpool will play their Champions League last-16 clash against Galatasaray without any away fans present after the Turkish side were hit with a UEFA punishment.

‌



Their fans have been banned from the game at Anfield, which will be the second leg of their knockout clash. It stems from fan misconduct charges that came about during last week's trip to Juventus, which included throwing objects, lighting fireworks and causing general disturbances.

‌



There reports of a man and his daughter sustaining injuries when a firework was launched towards Juventus fans in Turin. And as a result Europe's governing body have hit Galatasaray with a ban for their next European away day.

‌



As well as the ticket ban, UEFA's disciplinary panel have imposed a €40,000 fine on the Turkish champions for their fans' behaviour. They attempted to appeal against the aforementioned penalties, but they were rejected by UEFA's Appeals Body on Wednesday.

Galatasaray will host Liverpool in the first leg of the last-16 clash in Turkey on 10 March, but whatever happens they will make the trip to England without any travelling support.

Liverpool's predicted Premier League points tally in battle for Champions League place READ MORE :

That could prove crucial, especially if they have a lead to defend. Galatasaray are underdogs in the tie but they've shown they can mix it with some of the European heavyweights. In the knockout play-offs, they beat Juventus 7-5 on aggregate which saw them score twice in extra time.

‌



They've also been able to down Liverpool this season. That came when they hosted the Reds in Istanbul with Victor Osimhen scoring from the penalty spot to secure a memorable victory. Arne Slot has admitted: "We know in the away leg we will have to perform better than the last time we were in Istanbul back in September."

View 2 Images Galatasaray have been hit with a ban after the conduct of some of their fans in the game at Juventus

A strong run in the Champions League can help Liverpool salvage their season with the Premier League already beyond them. Their only two hopes of a trophy are on the European stage and in the FA Cup, where they face Wolves in the fifth round on Friday night.

Article continues below

Last term Liverpool, en route to the title, were knocked out of of the Champions League in the last 16. Eventual winners PSG beat them on penalties and one of Slot's tasks was to mount more of a charge on the European stage. If they see off Galatasaray, the Reds know they will face either the Parisians or Chelsea.

Football.title: Benjamin Sesko's classy gesture to Man Utd flop Rasmus Hojlund speaks volumes
Football.content:
Benjamin Sesko's classy gesture to Man Utd flop Rasmus Hojlund speaks volumes

Benjamin Sesko is showing why Manchester United paid £74million for his signature and were happy to loan out Rasmus Hojlund after two difficult seasons at Old Trafford

View 2 Images Benjamin Sesko is on fire at Manchester United (Image: Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images)

Benjamin Sesko refused to take the No.9 shirt at Manchester United out of respect for Rasmus Hojlund. Sesko, 22, joined the Red Devils in a deal worth up to £74million in August and was quickly installed as their first-choice striker.

‌



Holjlund, meanwhile, fell down the pecking order and it was clear that United wanted to offload to the Dane, two years after forking out £72m for him. But at the time of Sesko's arrival, Hojlund's loan move to Napoli - which was eventually confirmed on transfer deadline day - was far from a done deal.

‌



When it came time for Sesko to select his shirt number, the Athletic report that United considered allowing the Slovenia international to wait until Hojlund had departed. But with the 23-year-old still at the club, Sesko decided to take the No.30 out of respect for his then team-mate.

‌



Sesko, who's in red-form form under Michael Carrick, may well get the opportunity to take the No.9 this summer, as Napoli are obligated to sign Hojlund on a permanent basis if they qualify for the Champions League. The Serie A giants, who are currently third in the table, agreed on a £38million fee in September.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

The United loanee has suggested that he's keen to stay at Napoli, having won the Italian Super Cup and scored 13 goals in 34 games for Antonio Conte's side. In a recent interview with Italian publication Il Mattino, Hojlund said that he's keen to see more of the city of Naples and enjoys living there.

‌



"l'm not in the centre, but I'm dying to explore the alleys and heart of Naples, but I know it won't be easy to get around," the striker explained. "I sense the incredible energy the city exudes.

"I can feel it every moment - its vitality and personality are everywhere, not just at Maradona when we play. It's an underestimated city, not even a little dangerous, despite so many rumours from afar."

Should Manchester United bring back Rasmus Hojlund? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Napoli are likely to sign Rasmus Hojlund for £38million (Image: Image Photo Agency/Getty Images)

Hojlund has netted four goals in his last seven appearances after a quiet start to the calendar year. "I'm like ketchup. When the sauce doesn't come out, you're there, struggling to get it out, then suddenly it all comes out at once," he joked.

"It's the same when you don't score for a long time. It's an incredible joy to score, but when the team and the coach believe in you, the goal is like a flower that has to bloom, it's just a matter of time."

Football.title: Man Utd icon Wayne Rooney reveals big football regret after being 'battered' with abuse
Football.content:
Man Utd icon Wayne Rooney reveals big football regret after being 'battered' with abuse

Wayne Rooney has admitted one of his biggest regrets in football after being abused by a particular group of fans for one of the teams he supports

View 2 Images Former Manchester United forward Wayne Rooney (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United legend Wayne Rooney has revealed that failing to play for Celtic ranks among his biggest football regrets.

‌



Rooney, 40, enjoyed an illustrious career which featured 13 trophy-laden years at United where he racked up 559 appearances and netted 253 goals. During that period, the former England international became the club's all-time top scorer and secured 12 major honours, including five Premier League titles and one Champions League triumph.

‌



However, Rooney, who also turned out for Everton on two occasions and DC United before finishing his playing days at Derby County, has expressed his wish that he could have spent time north of the border at boyhood club Celtic.

‌



MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Man Utd boss Michael Carrick hints where he stands on Harry Maguire contract decision READ MORE :

When questioned on The Wayne Rooney Show about the finest atmosphere he has experienced, he responded: "Best atmosphere, I'd have to say Celtic Park. To play at Celtic in 2006, we actually lost the game 1-0, but to play there and feel that atmosphere was incredible."

Regarding the most hostile environment he encountered, Rooney stated: "Rangers away in 2010, I think it was. I'd played there for Everton in a pre-season game and I'm a big Celtic fan. I went to Ibrox to watch the Old Firm, to watch Celtic there, sat in the Celtic end, so I think they were aware of that.

‌



"So when we went in 2010, they battered me. It was not long after I'd asked to leave Manchester United and then I'd signed. So I was under pressure to perform for United again.

View 2 Images Wayne Rooney would have wanted to play for Celtic in his career (Image: Getty Images)

"We got a penalty, I've scored it and went off celebrating. It was a real hostile performance. I felt it against me, normally you feel it against the team, but I felt it against me.

‌



"Back then it was when I was using Twitter quite a bit and I was getting hammered on Twitter, so I was expecting it. I think it's well known and I've put out there that I'm a Celtic fan. It's probably one of my regrets, not playing for Celtic at some point."

Rooney, who was an Everton fan growing up, has always had a soft spot for Celtic, stating: "Just growing up as a kid, I've always liked Celtic.

"When I was growing up there was Henrik Larsson, Chris Sutton up top, John Hartson when he was there. Some of the players Celtic have had and moments they've given us, incredible. During the best part of my career, I always thought I'd love to play for Celtic at the end of my career. Obviously it wasn't to be."

Football.title: Marcus Rashford's behaviour brought up as Gary Lineker speaks out about Man Utd return
Football.content:
Marcus Rashford's behaviour brought up as Gary Lineker speaks out about Man Utd return

Gary Lineker has a strong view about Marcus Rashford's behaviour and his potential return to Manchester United

View 2 Images Marcus Rashford's Barcelona future is set to be resolved soon (Image: Getty Images)

Gary Lineker has pointed out Marcus Rashford’s impeccable behaviour at Barcelona while urging the forward not to return to Manchester United. Rashford, 28, joined the Catalan giants last summer on a season-long loan deal, having been frozen out of the United squad by Ruben Amorim.

‌



The England international has impressed during his time in Spain, managing 10 goals and 13 assists in 36 appearances across all competitions this season. His success with the La Liga side, who are currently leading the table, has made it increasingly likely that Barcelona will make his move permanent at the end of this season.

‌



The Blaugrana hold a £26million option to sign him in the summer, and it was reported by SPORT last weekend that the club have made a breakthrough in agreeing a salary with Rashford, should they reach an agreement with United.

‌



Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Amorim, who criticised Rashford’s application in training sessions, was sacked by United in January and replaced by Michael Carrick. With Carrick at the helm and United now thriving, some fans have urged the club to keep their talented academy graduate despite his Old Trafford career seemingly being over for more than a year.

However, former Barcelona striker Lineker has shut down that idea, given how well Rashford has settled in on and off the pitch in Catalonia, saying a return to United would only bring the British media circus on his back again. Along with advocating for him to stay at Barcelona, Lineker heaped praise on Rashford’s behaviour while suggesting he was unfairly targeted in England for his off-field incidents.

‌



Man Utd target stalls on signing new Dortmund deal to hold out for Premier League switch READ MORE :

Marcus Rashford situation changes at Barcelona after Man Utd transfer 'agreement' reached READ MORE :

Lineker said on The Rest Is Football podcast: "He’s not getting many minutes at the moment. That’s for obvious reasons. Lamine Yamal is playing on the right and Raphinha is playing on the left and they are both in the top five players in world football. So it’s understandable. But whenever he comes on he does really well."

Speaking about the likelihood of Rashford remaining at Barcelona, Lineker added: "I think that would be a great move for him, don’t you? It looks promising for him."

Podcast co-host Alex Aljoe shed light on Barcelona being impressed with his off-field behaviour even when he hasn’t been starting games. She replied: "Barcelona have really liked his attitude, the fact he hasn’t been starting week in week out but he’s delivered when he’s been brought on to the pitch.

‌



View 2 Images Gary Lineker says Rashford shouldn't return to parent club Manchester United (Image: The Rest is Football)

"His head’s in the right place. He’s not moaning or sulking when he’s not playing. He’s coming on and proving his quality when he’s given the opportunity.

"He was criticised at Manchester United for his attitude at times but it seems he’s really enjoying life in Barcelona and he’s really happy there. He’s clearly settling in well.

‌



"I think it would be a great move for him to make it permanent." Lineker agreed, saying: "I think he’s been really excellent in that [how he’s conducted himself]."

On the subject of whether United will keep him this summer, Lineker continued: "I think it will come down to Marcus. If he goes back to Manchester United he will face all the old criticism again.

"Oh, ‘He’s focusing too much on helping kids have food on their table’, like it’s a bad thing. They did this bizarre thing about why he was wearing some kind of flashy necklace.

Article continues below

"They don’t do that nonsense over there [in Spain] so he just gets on with his football. It was a fresh start and I would imagine he’s really loving it and not getting all the off-field nonsense he gets in this country. They leave you to live your life. It doesn’t compare to how our media is here."

Football.title: 'I was in Bruno Fernandes' situation – Man Utd should keep him if they want to win the title'
Football.content:
'I was in Bruno Fernandes' situation – Man Utd should keep him if they want to win the title'

Bruno Fernandes' future at Manchester United may be up in the air, but one Red Devils icon says the club should keep him

View 2 Images Bruno Fernandes' future remains up in the air (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United have been told they can clinch the Premier League title with Bruno Fernandes, but they must avoid the temptation to sell him despite links to three England stars. The Red Devils have experienced a revival under Michael Carrick and currently sit third in the table, boasting an 11-game unbeaten run.

‌



With each victory, their grip on Champions League qualification tightens, sparking hope among the United faithful that the groundwork for a potential title push next season is being laid.

‌



Central to their recent form has been Fernandes, who is in the running for PFA Player of the Year with seven goals and 13 assists to his name in just 25 league matches. The 31-year-old skipper is contracted until 2028, yet rumours of a possible summer departure persist.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Whilst the Portuguese ace is expected to make a decision on his future at the season's end, retaining the midfielder and resisting moves for Cole Palmer, Jude Bellingham or Morgan Rogers should be on United's to-do list, says club legend Bryan Robson.

Speaking to Boyle Sports, the ex-United captain stated: "Cole Palmer, Jude Bellingham and Morgan Rogers are all very, very good players but these lads are all attacking players. I think in the midfield area Manchester United need a really top player who can defend and pass the ball well. I think that's the type of player we need.

‌



Man Utd identify ANOTHER Premier League midfielder in search for Casemiro replacement READ MORE :

Michael Carrick's decision to scrap strict Ruben Amorim rule has impacted Bruno Fernandes READ MORE :

"Because of the signings like this year, with Sesko up front and Bruno in midfield, all those players, Palmer, Bellingham and Rogers, they have got great ability and great pace but they're not what United need."

Despite only having an FA Cup and Carabao Cup to his name, there are questions about whether Fernandes can steer United to a Premier League title. However, Robson has faith in Fernandes due to his experience and determination.

View 2 Images Bryan Robson urged Man Utd not to sign Cole Palmer, Morgan Rogers and Jude Bellingham (Image: James Baylis - AMA/Getty Images)

‌



The 69-year-old added: "Bruno Fernandes is a top player. He can do things on a football pitch other people can't. He creates chances for people. He scores great goals. I think his attitude is spot on. He's a great lad around the club.

"I was 33 when we won the FA Cup, then we went on to win the League Cup, we won the European Cup Winners' Cup, and then we won the title, and then we won the double. I was 33 when that all started up so when people keep talking about age.

"You can win a lot of things if you challenge yourself and keep yourself really fit. If you've got the experience then you can help people around you and I think Bruno can do that. The way Manchester United are playing at the moment."

Article continues below

Football.title: F1 bosses have already cancelled two races as worries grow over 2026 calendar
Football.content:
F1's stance on cancelling 2026 races as history shows calendar could be shook up

The 2026 Formula 1 season gets underway this weekend

View 2 Images The Saudi Arabia Grand Prix has found itself under scrutiny in the past (Image: Clive Rose, Getty Images)

Formula 1 has established a track record of calling off races when conflict or civil unrest erupts in the Middle East, although this season's schedule remains unchanged for now. A United States base in Bahrain has been shaken by an explosion amid rising tensions, prompting concerns about the forthcoming Grands Prix in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia.

‌



The 2026 season kicks off this weekend in Australia and will see drivers travel to Bahrain on April 12 and Saudi Arabia a week later. There are no immediate plans to remove those races from the calendar but F1 bosses have been flexible in the past amid calls for cancellations.

‌



Back in 2011, the Bahrain Grand Prix had been scheduled for March but was delayed amid anti-government demonstrations. The race had only joined the F1 calendar in 2004 but organisers believed they had little option but to defer the 2011 race.

‌



After initially being pushed back to October, it was eventually scrapped from the 2011 schedule entirely. The move came after former world champion Damon Hill criticised F1's determination to proceed with the event following the protests.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

F1 takes action to save Australian GP as two more races under threat from Middle East war READ MORE :

F1 'monitoring' Middle East situation amid upcoming Bahrain and Saudi Arabia GPs READ MORE :

"This crisis is an opportunity for Formula One to show that it cares about all people and their human rights," Hill said at the time. "I am not an official spokesperson for Formula One. I only speak for myself.

‌



"If Formula One agrees to race in Bahrain, it will forever have the blight of association with repressive methods to achieve order. The right thing to do, in my view, is to not race in Bahrain until these doubts have been removed."

Following the cancellation of the 2011 race, human rights campaigners issued fresh appeals to also strike Bahrain from the 2012 calendar. Those requests were disregarded, though, and the race proceeded as planned.

View 2 Images The Bahrain Grand Prix was called off in 2011 (Image: John Moore, Getty Images)

‌



The Saudi Arabia Grand Prix is a more recent inclusion, taking place for the first time in 2021. The year after, Yemen's Houthi rebel group targeted an oil facility just over 10 miles from the circuit in Jeddah during race weekend.

Drivers were participating in Friday practice that very day, with Max Verstappen expressing concerns to engineer Gianpiero Lambiase that he could smell smoke. He received assurances that his car wasn't the source of the smell, though that didn't stop some concerns being raised about the race considering the backdrop.

These worries were ultimately addressed, with the Grand Prix Drivers Association subsequently addressing the situation. "Yesterday was a difficult day for Formula One and a stressful day for us Formula One drivers," their statement read.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

"Perhaps it is difficult to comprehend if you have never driven an F1 car on this fast and challenging Jeddah track, but on seeing the smoke from the incident, it was difficult to remain a fully focused race driver and erase natural human concerns. Consequently, we went into long discussions between ourselves, with our team principals, and with the most senior people who run our sport.

"A large variety of opinions were shared and debated and, having listened not only to the Formula 1 powers but also to the Saudi government minister who explained how security measures were being elevated to the maximum, the outcome was a resolution that we would practice and qualify today and race tomorrow. We therefore hope that the 2022 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix will be remembered as a good race rather than for the incident that took place yesterday."

Football.title: Liverpool's predicted Premier League points tally in battle for Champions League place
Football.content:
Liverpool's predicted Premier League points tally in battle for Champions League place

Liverpool were beaten 2-1 by Wolves on Tuesday night and Opta predictions now tip the Reds to finish fifth on 62 points as Champions League qualification race heats up

View 2 Images Liverpool are fighting to finish in the Champions League places this season (Image: Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

Liverpool were left reeling by a stoppage-time defeat to Wolves on Tuesday night. The 2-1 reverse was a squandered opportunity to, at least temporarily, climb back into the top four.

‌



Wolves' Rodrigo Gomes broke the deadlock in the 78th minute before Mohamed Salah levelled five minutes later. But midfielder Andre delivered a decisive strike in stoppage time, and Chelsea now have the opportunity to narrow the gap and pull level on 48 points.

‌



Liam Rosenior's team face Aston Villa tonight (Wednesday) at Villa Park as they seek to recover after their 2-1 defeat to Arsenal last weekend. Following the setback at Molineux, Opta Analyst have issued a fresh prediction for how they anticipate the final table to appear on May 24.

‌



Trent Alexander-Arnold gets thumbs up to complete Liverpool return amid Anfield issues READ MORE :

Virgil van Dijk points finger after Liverpool fall to embarrassing Wolves defeat READ MORE :

The Reds have been tipped to finish fifth in the Premier League on 62 points (62.97), with Manchester United predicted to conclude the campaign three points ahead in fourth on 65.85. Despite Michael Carrick's outfit climbing above Aston Villa after their 2-1 comeback victory against Crystal Palace, they are still projected to finish fourth.

A fifth-place finish could still guarantee Liverpool a Champions League berth next season.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



England currently sit atop UEFA's coefficient table on 113.130 points, with all nine clubs still active in European competition.

In the Champions League, Arne Slot's side have been given a 36.6 per cent chance of winning the tournament. At the top of the table, the title race is poised to be a nail-biter, with a mere five points currently separating Arsenal and Manchester City.

View 2 Images Liverpool currently sit fifth in the Premier League (Image: PA)

‌



Pep Guardiola's team have a match in hand over Arsenal, with their fixture against Crystal Palace needing to be rearranged due to their Carabao Cup final clash with the Gunners later this month.

Nevertheless, Arsenal are tipped to clinch the Premier League title and finish five points ahead of City on 82 points (82.71). Their odds of being crowned champions stand at 83.65 percent, whilst City's are at 16.29 percent. Consequently, they are projected to finish second with 77 points (77.21).

As anticipated, Arsenal are also deemed to have a 100 percent chance of securing a spot in the Champions League next season, with a 19‐point buffer to sixth place.

Article continues below

At the opposite end of the table, West Ham, Burnley and Wolves are predicted to face relegation.

Football.title: Ex-Premier League star's wife rushed to hospital after car flips over during school run
Football.content:
Ex-Premier League star's wife rushed to hospital after car flips over during school run

She was treated for injuries and joined in hospital by her famous husband after the accident

View 2 Images Marlene Alvarenga's car was seen flipped over after an accident in Turkey

The wife of former Premier League footballer Nelson Semedo has been taken to hospital following a car crash in Turkey. Marlene Alvarenga is reported to have been on her way to collect her children from school when the incident occurred.

‌



Former Wolves and Barcelona right-back Semedo departed Molineux last summer after five years at the club. He signed for Fenerbahce on a free transfer, putting pen to paper on a two-year contract.

‌



According to Portuguese outlet O Jogo, Alvarenga was not driving at the time of the crash on the Northern Marmara Motorway, to the north of Istanbul. Reports suggest that the unnamed driver lost control of the vehicle, which then flipped onto its side.

‌



Semedo, 32, rushed to his wife's side at the hospital upon hearing of the collision. She is understood to have sustained only minor injuries.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Virgil van Dijk points finger after Liverpool fall to embarrassing Wolves defeat READ MORE :

"We are saddened to learn of the traffic accident involving Marlene Semedo, the wife of our football player Nelson Semedo, and we send our best wishes to her and her family," his club said in a statement. The footballer and his wife have a daughter and a son together.

‌



Semedo is currently out of action due to an injury he suffered while playing for Fenerbahce. The club confirmed he suffered medial ligament damage following a recent match against Antalyaspor.

The Portugal international was withdrawn just 20 minutes into the fixture, which ended all square. Domenico Tedesco's squad remain undefeated in the league following 24 matches but occupy second spot, four points behind Galatasaray.

View 2 Images Marlene Alvarenga with Nelson Semedo

‌



He has also featured regularly for the club in Europe, where Fener were defeated by Benfica in the Champions League play-offs before reaching the knockout play-offs in the Europa League, losing 4-2 on aggregate to Nottingham Forest.

Semedo, who played for Barcelona before his five-year Wolves spell, was brought to Turkey by then-manager Jose Mourinho over the summer. Mourinho was dismissed in August following that Champions League elimination, however, with Tedesco assuming control.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Fenerbahce have confirmed manager Tedesco will be absent from this week's cup fixture against Gaziantep. The German boss is "undergoing treatment for a severe infection," according to a club statement.

Victory over Gaziantep would guarantee Fenerbahce reach the quarter-finals of the cup. The two teams are level following three group stage matches, with Gaziantep ahead on goals scored, though the two best third-placed sides will also advance.

Football.title: Fletcher apologises for use of homophobic slur after six-match ban
Football.content:
Man Utd's Jack Fletcher banned for six matches and fined after admitting using homophobic slur towards an opponent in Vertu Trophy match in October. Warning: This article contains content some readers may find upsetting

Jack Fletcher, son of Darren Fletcher, has received a six-match ban for using a homophobic slur in an academy game against Barnsley

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player

Manchester United midfielder Jack Fletcher has apologised for using a homophobic slur during a Vertu Trophy match after being handed a six-game ban.

Fletcher, 18, the son of former United midfielder and current coach Darren Fletcher, was sent off in the incident, which occurred in a 5-2 win against Barnsley in October.

Fletcher was heard by the referee directing a homophobic term at an opponent, which Sky Sports is aware of but has taken the decision not to repeat.

When the charge was brought, the FA said: "It is alleged that during the fixture, you acted in an improper manner and/or used abusive and/or insulting words, contrary to FA Rule E3.1.

"It is alleged that the breach of FA Rule E3.1 is an "Aggravated Breach", as defined in FA Rule E3.2, as it included a reference, whether express or implied, to sexual orientation."

An FA regulatory commission hearing on February 3 decided he had used it as an insult and handed Fletcher a six-match ban, fined him £1,500 and also ordered him to complete an education order.

Image: Jack Fletcher was fined £1,500 and banned for six matches by the FA after admitting using a homophobic slur towards an opponent

"I am truly sorry for the offensive word that I used in the heat of the moment," Fletcher said.

"Despite the fact I had no intention to use the term as a homophobic insult, I completely understand that such language is unacceptable and immediately apologised after the game.

"I want to be clear that this momentary lapse of character absolutely does not reflect my beliefs or values."

A statement on social media from Rainbow Devils, United's LGBTQ+ supporters' club, welcomed Fletcher's apology.

"We hope Jack learns from this and grows as a person and a player," it read.

"His immediate regret and guilty plea suggests that he will."

A statement from United read: "Manchester United has worked with Jack to strengthen his understanding of discriminatory language and why it is harmful.

"In addition to his ongoing participation in regular academy programmes on diversity and inclusion, Jack will also take part in educational training through the FA (Football Association).

"Manchester United prides itself on being an inclusive and welcoming club. Since launching All Red All Equal in 2016, we have embedded equality, diversity and inclusion into everything we do."

Football.title: Donald given historic third stint as Europe's Ryder Cup captain
Football.content:
Luke Donald will captain Team Europe for a third consecutive Ryder Cup after being named in the role again for the 2027 contest at Adare Manor in Ireland.

Donald has guided Team Europe to back-to-back victories over the United States, leading them to a 16.5-11.5 win in Rome in 2023 - avenging a record-breaking loss two years earlier - before successfully retaining the trophy in New York last September.

Team Europe built a record-breaking lead over Team USA over the first two days at Bethpage Black before closing out a 15-13 victory, seeing Donald join Tony Jacklin as the only European captains to win successive Ryder Cups.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the key moments from the final day of the Ryder Cup from Bethpage Black, as Europe held on to clinch the trophy

The former world No 1 was described as the 'best European captain of all time' after their famous win on American soil, with the former world No 1 now confirmed to become the first since Bernard Gallacher to captain Europe in three consecutive Ryder Cups.

Donald will look to defend the trophy once again on home soil, live next September on Sky Sports, where he aims to complete a historic three-peat and become the first European to win three Ryder Cups as a captain.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Team USA captain Keegan Bradley heaped praise on Luke Donald after Team Europe clinched the 2025 Ryder Cup

"The last two Ryder Cups have meant a lot to me and my family," Donald said. "I didn't imagine this third time would come. Celebrating on that Sunday night in New York after a pressure-packed week in a tough environment, I thought maybe my job was done. But maybe there is a little more story to tell.

"This Ryder Cup captaincy journey has given me so much focus, so much purpose and it is something I don't take for granted. It's a real privilege and I am certainly looking forward to another home Ryder Cup."

Captain Donald continues historic journey

Donald has never been on a losing team in the Ryder Cup as a player or a captain, appearing in four victorious sides as a player before masterminding the back-to-back wins over the United States as a captain.

The Englishman was part of winning teams as a player in 2004, 2006, 2010 and 2012, with Donald also serving as a vice-captain to Thomas Bjorn in the 2018 success in Paris and Padraig Harrington in the heavy 2021 loss at Whistling Straits.

Image: Luke Donald has been named captain for the 2027 Ryder Cup at Adare Manor

Donald was initially a replacement captain for the 2023 edition, following Henrik Stenson being stripped from the role for joining LIV Golf, although has since cemented his status as one of Europe's Ryder Cup greats.

He was met with a chant of "two more years" from his players after the Rome victory, which he responded to by taking on the role again for the 2025 edition, with a similar message directed his way after last year's title defence.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Matt Fitzpatrick says he 'wouldn't blame' Donald if he stepped down as Team Europe Ryder Cup captain, but made it clear that the team 'would all welcome him back'

He held discussions with DP World Tour chief executive Guy Kinnings in January and has been locked in as captain again for the centenary edition at Limerick's Adare Manor, taking place from September 17-19 next year.

"History is obviously important to me," Donald added. "As a team, as Ryder Cup Europe, we all play for history. We talk about it a lot, about the guys who paved the way for us and the responsibility we have to inspire next generations.

Twitter Twitter , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Twitter cookies or to allow those cookies just once. "I don't think I have ever thought about history through a personal lens. I just try to enjoy the journey and the day-to-day work to create an environment that gives the players the opportunity for success. That is what I focus on."

Donald had previously refused to rule out a third stint in the role immediately after ending home dominance in the biennial contest, with victory at Bethpage Black the first away win - from either team - since the 2012 'Miracle at Medinah'.

Twitter Twitter , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. Guy Kinnings, chief executive of the European Tour Group, said: "Luke was an outstanding captain in Rome and New York, leading the team to victory both times, and it is fantastic he is coming back to do it a third time.

"Those victories were remarkable, but almost even more impressive was how he led the team and how he conducted himself. He has faced a lot of challenges as captain throughout his two terms, and he has handled all of them with his usual calm, poise and authority and above all with respect.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Team Europe carried on their celebrations on the team bus following their Ryder Cup victory, sending their own message to US President Donald Trump

"He is meticulous in everything he does when it comes to planning and preparation, but Luke would be the first to acknowledge that as good as job as he has done, this is a new challenge for him and the team. He will be as motivated and as committed as ever to help them achieve more success."

The PGA of America are yet to announce who will captain Team USA for the 2027 contest, with Tiger Woods the favourite to succeed Keegan Bradley after turning down the opportunity last time around.

Watch the next two Ryder Cups, including the 2027 edition at Adare Manor in Ireland, exclusively live on Sky Sports. Stream the DP World Tour and more with no contract.
